task_completion.description: Verify that Stellarium is set to the correct location and historical time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Stellarium Date/Time window showing '1805-10-21' and '20:00:00', and the Location window or bottom toolbar showing coordinates near 36.18° N, 6.03° W at 0m altitude.

task_completion.description: Verify that all requested visual toggles are correctly set in the sky view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the sky view: stars should be visible below the horizon line (ground disabled), mythological constellation artwork must be drawn, a circular azimuthal coordinate grid must be visible, and cardinal direction labels (N, S, E, W) must be shown.

task_completion.description: Verify that a screenshot of the Moon was taken.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot centered on or clearly displaying the Moon, rendered with Stellarium's graphics.

task_completion.description: Verify that a screenshot of Jupiter was taken.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot centered on or clearly displaying Jupiter, rendered with Stellarium's graphics.

task_completion.description: Verify that a wide-field screenshot of the sky was taken.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a zoomed-out screenshot showing a broad view of the sky with the requested grids and artwork visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the reference notes file with the celestial positions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal showing the file '/home/ga/Desktop/trafalgar_sky_notes.txt' containing positional data (such as RA/Dec or Az/Alt coordinates) for both the Moon and Jupiter.

integrity.description: Verify that the screenshots are actual Stellarium outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should feature Stellarium's specific UI elements, rendering style, constellation art, and azimuthal grid, rather than being generic images of the night sky downloaded from the internet.

integrity.description: Verify that the notes file contains actual recorded data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file should contain specific astronomical coordinates (numbers/degrees) for the Moon and Jupiter, not just a copy-paste of the task description or placeholder text.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent attempted to save the required files to the Desktop.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the file manager or terminal to ensure the screenshots and the 'trafalgar_sky_notes.txt' file are located in or moved to the /home/ga/Desktop/ directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires setting Stellarium to Cape Trafalgar (36.18° N, 6.03° W, altitude 0m) on October 21, 1805, at 20:00:00 UTC. The sky view must have the atmosphere ON, landscape/ground OFF, constellation art ON, azimuthal grid ON, and cardinal directions ON. The agent must capture screenshots of the Moon, Jupiter, and a wide-field view, and record the positions of the Moon and Jupiter in '/home/ga/Desktop/trafalgar_sky_notes.txt'.
